suggestions.topic: glamsterdam-devnet-6 Gloas fork stability and devnet-7 readiness
suggestions.rationale: devnet-6 launched just before #181 with Gloas fork activation during the call. Stability assessment is critical before proceeding to devnet-7 (targeted mid-July per #181). EIP-7688 DFI decision is contingent on devnet-6 stability.
Deferred from #179 for a two-week revisit (expected at #180 or #181 but not addressed). Already implemented and tested since devnet-1 with intent to SFI. Overdue for final stage decision.

suggestions.topic: EIP-7688 (progressive containers) DFI decision for Glamsterdam
suggestions.rationale: Targeted for devnet-7 in #181 but not DFI'd, with decision deferred pending devnet-6 stability. devnet-7 is ~2–3 weeks out (mid-July), so DFI decision is needed now to finalize scope.

suggestions.topic: EIP-8282 (builder deposit contract) contract finalization and audit scheduling
suggestions.rationale: Updated contract (fee-per-enqueue) targets devnet-7 with external audit scheduled for end of July. Action from #181 requires PR for new contract constants (target=8, max=64) and signal for audit readiness. Need confirmation on contract finalization status.

Multiple Beacon API PRs were assigned for review by ACDT in #181. PR #618 (validator state rename) from #180 is a breaking change requiring broader ecosystem engagement. Need confirmation on review completion and merge readiness.

suggestions.topic: ePBS circuit breaker implementation status across clients
suggestions.rationale: #181 decided circuit breakers should track missing payloads (not beacon blocks) with client-specific implementations acceptable. Prysm was using incorrect consensus-block-based breaker. Need status updates on client implementations before devnet-7.

suggestions.topic: Hegota headliner proposals: EIP-8148, EIP-8243, EIP-8025 status
suggestions.rationale: EIP-8148 (custom sweep threshold) proposed for Hegota in #179 with client review requested but not followed up in later calls. EIP-8243 (pre-aggregating attestations) presented in #180. EIP-8025 (optional execution proofs) from #178 had no subsequent discussion. Need clarity on Hegota scope decisions.

Potuz flagged in #180 that all clients use canonical head only for PTC duties, creating liveness risk during network forks. Issue was not revisited in #181. May warrant discussion if devnet-6 reveals related issues.
